reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 1.5% and 20-day momentum is 11.3%.
Trend quality is supported by 60% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 3.8% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 1.5%, downside volatility near 1.2%, and a recent max drawdown of 2.5%.
Volume confirmation is -32.7% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 3.3% versus SPY over 5 days and 1.3% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.68, confidence 0.42, and 0.4 weighted articles across 3 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.83, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 3 articles across 3 sources, 0.4 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.87, average source quality 0.82, confidence 0.76.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC 0.00).
Overall model score is 0.093 with medium confidence.

news_evidence.title: JPMorgan Chase unveils $50 billion buyback, Goldman Sachs raises dividend after Fed stress test
news_evidence.llm_reason: Direct, material corporate action: a $50B buyback is a large capital return likely to support the share price short-term. Context of Fed stress test (banks passed) further reduces regulatory uncertainty. High likelihood of near-term positive price impact.
